claim: Software development lifecycle has seven stages: requirements, design, planning, build, review, deploy, and monitor; AI accelerates all stages, not just coding
actionable: Apply AI to every stage of SDLC: use it for requirements gathering, design documentation, task decomposition, code review, deployment scripting, and monitoring analysis
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: Skipping planning stages leads to poor AI coding results; spending 3+ days on requirements and design before coding yields much better outcomes
actionable: Invest significant time upfront in requirements and design before coding; don't jump to implementation
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: Spec-driven development: provide AI agents with complete technical design specs including libraries, frameworks, security considerations, and implementation details
actionable: Write detailed technical specs before briefing coding agents; include framework choices, security requirements, and architectural decisions
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: AI agents should break large work into smaller, testable tasks rather than attempting monolithic implementations; smaller scopes reduce agent errors and enable verification at each step
actionable: Decompose requirements into small, independently verifiable tasks before delegating to AI agents
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: Use task management systems (not markdown) to manage agent work; MCPs allow direct task delegation from systems like Linear
actionable: Use proper project management tools (Linear, Jira, etc.) with MCP integrations; avoid markdown-based task tracking
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: Never accept the first pass of AI-generated code; ask agents to self-review for errors, edge cases, security vulnerabilities, and missing input validation
actionable: Always request self-review; ask agents to find errors before you accept their code
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: AI agents can help with deployment and infrastructure: debugging cloud provider configs, writing Terraform, running Git commands, and diagnosing permission errors
actionable: Use AI agents for deployment troubleshooting; let them debug cloud provider issues and write infrastructure-as-code
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)

claim: Build automated code review into CI/CD pipelines; AI agents can review pull requests and provide feedback automatically
actionable: Set up AI code review agents in GitHub Actions or equivalent; trigger automated review on pull requests
source_title: The Full AI Development Workflow (Not Just Code)
